title: pelargonium fergusoniae
intro: Pelargonium fergusoniae, a species of flowering plant within the family Geraniaceae, is commonly known as the 'Cape Rose'. It is native to the Western Cape region of South Africa, where it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing digestive issues. The herbal preparation of Pelargonium fergusoniae can be found in various forms, including teas, extracts, and infusions, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
